PA1_template.Rmd

Will S.

3/10/2021

This markdown file would explain the code and reasoning of the code. ###Question 1. Loading and preprocessing the data Show any code that is needed to: Load the data (i.e. read.csv()) Process/transform the data (if necessary) into a format suitable for your analysis

First set the working directory to the desktop, where the data is. Load relevant libraries. And read them. Use ymd from lubridate to modify the format of the date.

setwd("~/Desktop")
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(lubridate)
## 
## Attaching package: 'lubridate'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     date, intersect, setdiff, union
act_file <- read.csv("activity.csv")
act_file$date <- ymd(act_file$date)
act_file_clean <- act_file[complete.cases(act_file),]

###Question 2. What is mean total number of steps taken per day? histogram of the total number of steps taken each day

sub1 <- group_by(act_file_clean[,1:2], date) %>% 
  summarize(totalsteps = sum(steps))
png("plot1.png", width = 480, height = 480)
with(sub1, barplot(totalsteps))
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2
with(sub1, barplot(totalsteps))

Calculate and report the mean and median total number of steps taken per day

sub2<- group_by(act_file_clean[,1:2], date) %>% 
  summarize(mean = mean(steps), median = median(steps))
sub2
## # A tibble: 53 x 3
##    date         mean median
##  * <date>      <dbl>  <dbl>
##  1 2012-10-02  0.438      0
##  2 2012-10-03 39.4        0
##  3 2012-10-04 42.1        0
##  4 2012-10-05 46.2        0
##  5 2012-10-06 53.5        0
##  6 2012-10-07 38.2        0
##  7 2012-10-09 44.5        0
##  8 2012-10-10 34.4        0
##  9 2012-10-11 35.8        0
## 10 2012-10-12 60.4        0
## # … with 43 more rows

###Question 3. What is the average daily activity pattern? Time series plot of 5-min interval and avg number of steps by days

png("plot2.png", width = 480, height = 480)
with(sub2, plot(date,mean,type="l"))
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2
with(sub2, plot(date,mean,type="l"))

The interval having the max steps

sub3 <- group_by(act_file_clean, interval) %>% 
  summarize(steps=max(steps)) 
maxinterval <- sub3 %>% 
  filter(steps==max(sub3$steps)) %>% 
  select(interval)
maxinterval
## # A tibble: 1 x 1
##   interval
##      <int>
## 1      615

###Question 4. Imputing NAs

nafile <- act_file[!complete.cases(act_file),]
nafile$steps <- 0
act_imputing <- rbind(nafile, act_file_clean)

Histogram with imputed figures.

sub7 <- group_by(act_imputing[,1:2], date) %>% 
  summarize(totalsteps = sum(steps))
png("plot3.png", width = 480, height = 480)
with(sub7, barplot(totalsteps))
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2
with(sub7, barplot(totalsteps))

mean and median with the imputed

sub7_2<- group_by(act_imputing[,1:2], date) %>% 
  summarize(mean = mean(steps), median = median(steps))

sub7_2
## # A tibble: 61 x 3
##    date         mean median
##  * <date>      <dbl>  <dbl>
##  1 2012-10-01  0          0
##  2 2012-10-02  0.438      0
##  3 2012-10-03 39.4        0
##  4 2012-10-04 42.1        0
##  5 2012-10-05 46.2        0
##  6 2012-10-06 53.5        0
##  7 2012-10-07 38.2        0
##  8 2012-10-08  0          0
##  9 2012-10-09 44.5        0
## 10 2012-10-10 34.4        0
## # … with 51 more rows

###Question 5. panel plot

library(lattice)
act_file_clean$wday <- wday(act_file_clean$date)
act_file_clean<-act_file_clean %>% 
  mutate(wd = wday>=6)
sub8 <- act_file_clean[,c(1,3,5)]
wkdy <- sub8 %>% 
  filter(wd=="FALSE") %>%
  select(steps, interval) %>% 
  group_by(interval) %>% 
  summarize(steps=mean(steps)) %>%
  mutate(id = "weekday")
wked <- sub8 %>% 
  filter(wd=="TRUE") %>%
  select(steps, interval) %>% 
  group_by(interval) %>% 
  summarize(steps=mean(steps)) %>% 
  mutate(id = "weekend")
sub8 <- rbind(wkdy, wked)
png("plot4.png", width = 480, height = 480)
xyplot(steps ~ interval | id, data = sub8, layout = c(1, 2), type= "l")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2
xyplot(steps ~ interval | id, data = sub8, layout = c(1, 2), type= "l")
